body: In some configurations, we observed pressure inversions at low elevations. These are problematic because they prevent the conversion between pressure and altitudes. This commit remove the region of space that we empirically found being the culprit. If pressure inversions occur somewhere else, an error will sill be thrown.
title: Window elevations to avoid pressure inversions

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
All config files now use one of the following 2 config files: `"config/longrun_configs/amip_target_diagedmf.yml"` or `"config/longrun_configs/amip_target_edonly.yml"` , with the exception of benchmark runs which use atmos config files defined within ClimaCoupler.jl. I think this is fine since we look at those runs for performance more than for physics. The DYAMOND run is also unchanged because we expect to remove it this week. Some variables are overwritten, so we don't always have the same final atmosphere configuration. But we will now use a standardized set of TOML parameters, which should help with debugging.

Config files are also alphabetized in this PR (in a separate commit), since they had gotten out of order.
title: standardize atmos configs
